findings.title: Base64 decode passed to exec
findings.message: Decoded base64 content is executed dynamically. Classic obfuscated payload execution.

findings.title: Downloaded payload written then executed
findings.message: Network content is persisted as Python code and then executed. Classic dropper pattern.

findings.title: Environment variable exfiltrated via HTTP POST
findings.message: Sensitive environment variable (API_TOKEN) collected then sent to a remote endpoint. Credential theft pattern.

findings.title: eval() on a reconstructed joined string
findings.message: String fragments joined at runtime and passed to eval(). Common obfuscation to evade static string matching.

findings.title: Dynamic import used to invoke system command
findings.message: importlib.import_module used to load 'os' dynamically, then system() called. Bypasses static import analysis.

findings.title: Command injection via string concatenation in os.system
findings.message: User-controlled value concatenated into a shell command string. Enables arbitrary command injection.

findings.title: Pickle deserialization of untrusted network bytes
findings.message: pickle.loads() called on raw bytes from an HTTP response. Allows remote code execution via crafted payload.

findings.title: Reversed string literal passed to exec
findings.message: A reversed string is reconstructed at runtime and passed to exec(). Obfuscation to evade static string analysis.

findings.title: Stage-2 loader: writes .py file then imports it
findings.message: Script writes Python source to disk then dynamically imports it. Enables persistent stage-2 payload execution.

findings.title: ctypes.CDLL loads a base64-decoded library path
findings.message: A base64-encoded path is decoded at runtime and loaded as a native library via ctypes. Shellcode or rootkit delivery pattern.
